NO LATER THAN NOON ON JULY 10, 2019 JUDGEMENT DEBTOR TO SUBMIT A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF ADDRESSING IN DETAIL THE PROCEDURE FOR THE JUDGMENT CREDITOR IS TO FOLLOW TO COLLECT THE UNDERTAKING IN THE EVENT SURETIES FAIL TO VOLUNTARILY COVER THE UNDERTAKING. THE BRIEF SHALL SEPARATELY COVER EACH INTEREST IN EACH PROPERTY BY EACH SURETY. JUDGEMENT CREDITOR MAY FILE A SUPPLEMENTAL REPLY BY JULY 11, 2019 TO ADDRESS THE SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION. ADDITIONALLY, PARTIES SHOULD MEET AND CONFER TO AGREE ON A DISINTERESTED PERSON TO APPRAISE THE VALUE OF THE PROPERTIES AND THE VALUE OF THE SURETIES' INTERESTS IN THE PROPERTIES. IF PARTIES CANNOT AGREE ON AN INDEPENDENT APPRAISER, EACH PARTY TO PROVIDE NAMES OF AT LEAST 1 (NOT MORE THAN 3) INDEPENDENT APPRAISERS READY AND WILLING TO CONDUCT THE APPRAISAL IMMEDIATELY BY NOON ON JULY 11, 2019. PARTIES TO PROVIDE A TIME ESTIMATE FOR THE APPRAISAL. ALL SURETIES TO BE PRESENT AT THE HEARING ON JULY 18, 2019. THE COURT MAY CONDUCT AN EVIDENTIARY HEARING. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFIED. COUNSELS INFORMED VIA TELEPHONE. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: A. PASCUAL, REPORTER: NANCY E. PRESANT (510-757-8550), CSR 	906, NPRESANTCSR@GMAIL.COM, COURT REPORTER. (D501)

Contested by plaintiffs and defendants. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RECEIVER KEVIN WHELAN'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER 1) APPROVING RECEIVER'S FINAL ACCOUNT AND REPORT, 2) APPROVING RECEIVER FEES AND EXPENSES, 3) APPROVING DISTRIBUTION OF FUNDS, 4) DISCHARGING RECEIVER, 5) EXONERATING BOND, 6) REQUIRING COURT PERMISSION AND FUNDING THE RECEIVERSHIP IN ORDER TO FILE CLAIM THAT INVOLVES RECEIVER, 7) RETAINING JURISDICTION RE MATTERS OR CLAIMS INVOLVING RECEIVER, 8) AUTHORIZING PLAINTIFF TO OBTAIN RESIDUAL PROCEEDS FROM CASE CGC-18-564941 is GRANTED as proposed by the receiver, except for the distribution to the Plaintiff. Distribution to the Plaintiffs at this time shall be limited to the remainder of funds in the estate after $500,000 is transferred to the receiver, Kevin Singer in Umpqua action, to be held in reserve to cover Umpqua's incurred and potential fees to litigate 5 outstanding appeals and outstanding trial level attorney's fees previously awarded by the Court. The Court makes no determination as to the final amount of fees or entitlement to fees on appeal at this time. This issue will be addressed upon the conclusion of the appeals and any interested parties will be entitled to submit their claims to the reserve funds thereafter. Untimely peremptory challenge filed on February 23, 2022 as to the hearing first noticed for December 7, 2021 to be heard by the Honorable Charles F. Haines (filed and served on November 3, 2021) is DENIED.

Receiver to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Dina Thompson, CSR 696, CSR.DinaThompson@gmail.com, 707-592-7733. (D501)

No appearance by Defendant/Judgement Debtor Anne Kihage.

Plaintiffs' Motion to Enforce Orders for Debtors Examination, and to Overrule Defendant(s) Objection(s) - GRANTED.
Defendants' Requests for an automatic stay, and/or for a temporary stay pending briefing
on a motion for stay, and/or for other continuance of these matters, DENIED.

Order GRANTING Plaintiff/Judgment Creditor Dale Duncan's Motion pertains to the following OEXs:
1) Order to Appear to Anne Kihage as managing member of Judgment Debtor Zoriall
LLC, which OEX was issued on February 11, 2019;
2) Order to Appear to Anne Kihage as an individual Judgment Debtor, which OEX was
issued on February 11, 2019;
3) Order to Appear to Anne Kihage as managing member of Renka Prop LLC, which
OEX was issued on February 15, 2019; and

Court GRANTS Plaintiff/Judgment Creditor Dale Duncan's request of issuance of Notice and Order continuing her OEX to a date in June 2022.
4) Order to Appear to third party Lin Dee Liu, which OEX was issued on February 15,
2019.

The Court takes Judicial Notice that Defendant/Judgment Debtor Anne Kihage can fly from Los Angeles to San Francisco in an hour.

If Defendant/Judgment Debtor Anne Kihage does not appear in-person tomorrow, February 7, 2022 at 2:00 p.m., for the Order to Show Cause re: OEX, in Department 514, the issued Stay will be lifted.

Counsel for Plaintiff/Judgment Creditor shall prepare an amended Proposed Order consistent with Court's Order and shall submit same for Court's review and signature. A copy to be provided to the opposing counsel.

Argued. The brief shall separately cover each interest in each property by each surety. Judgement creditor may file a supplemental reply by July 11, 2019 to address the supplemental opposition. Additionally, parties should meet and confer to agree on a disinterested person to appraise the value of the properties and the value of the sureties' interests in the properties. If parties cannot agree on an independent appraiser, each party to provide names of at least 1 (not more than 3) independent appraisers ready and willing to conduct the appraisal immediately by noon on July 11, 2019. Parties to provide a time estimate for the appraisal. All sureties to be present at the hearing on July 18, 2019. The Court may conduct an evidentiary hearing. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as modified. Counsels informed via telephone. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: A. Pascual, Reporter: Nancy E. Judgment Debtor's Motion For Appointment Of Receiver.

Hearing Re: Receiver Appointment And Fees. (Specially Set At 9am Before Judge Kahn)
Off Calendar Mar-01-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ariel Gershon, Esq, 415-773-1240 x351 of Greenstein & McDonald for Plaintiff. Richard Diestel, Esq. and Ariana A. Cardoza, Esq. 415-981-5411 of Bledsoe for Defendant Anne Kihagi.

Ruling - A R G U E D. The Court itemizes the errors and raises the issue of Mr. Whelan as biased. Defendant argues. (As fully reported in the reporter's notes) The court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Hearing re: Receiver appointment and fees, (specially set at 9am before Judge Kahn)

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiffs and judgment creditors Dale Duncan and Marta Mendoza's motion for appointment of Kevin Whelan as receiver and for a restraining order is granted. Plaintiffs, as judgment creditors of a large judgment, have shown good cause for the appointment of a limited receiver for the collection and payment to them of proceeds of the sales of real property assets of defendants and judgment debtors Anne Kihagi, Zoriall LLC, and Christina Mwangi. Except for the language on line 3 of page ten of their proposed order, plaintiffs have also shown good cause for their requested restraining order, which is in aid of their efforts to enforce their judgment. Plaintiffs' proposed restraining order is sufficiently precise and, but for the prohibition against communications with him or his employees and persons retained by him, is a measured restraint that is necessary and proper to enable the receiver to carry out his duties. Mr. Whelan is qualified to serve as the receiver. Defendants have not shown that Mr. Whelan is biased, will act prejudicially against their interests, or otherwise not follow the directions of the court. However, defendants may raise, on an ex parte basis if urgency is necessary, any objections they have about any actual or contemplated action of Mr. Whelan in the event that Mr. Whelan does act or proposes to act in a manner that shows he is biased, prejudiced against their interests, or otherwise not in compliance with the directions of the court. The fact that Mr. Whelan has consulted with and assisted plaintiffs' counsel with this motion and related matters does not, without more, show that he is unfit to be a receiver for this case. As the intended beneficiaries of the appointment of the receiver, plaintiffs' choice of receiver is entitled to great weight and there has been no showing sufficient to overcome the presumption that Mr. Whelan will faithfully comply with his oath and all court orders. Mr. Whelan must file a receiver's bond of $10,000 no later than March 8, 2019. (As fully reported in the reporter's transcript) Two orders are signed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ariel Gershon, Esq., Greenstein & Mcdonald, Telephone 415-773-1240, appearing for Plaintiff,

Richards S. Diestel, Esq., Bledsoe, Diestel, Treppa & Crane, LLP, Telephone 415-381-5411, appearing for Defendant;

Ruling - Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Judgment debtor Anne Kihagi's motion to seal any issued charging orders is denied. The right of public access to court records is at its greatest with regard to court orders. A transparent and fully accountable court system cannot function if court orders were not accessible to the public. Ms. Kihagi has failed to show any of the facts required by CRC 2.550(d) for the issuance of the order she seeks. Parties to submit by email a proposed order and objection to proposed order by end of day on Friday 12-14-18.

The Court announces the Tentative ruling. Both sides ARGUED. Defendant contests as to COA for harassment; 37.10(b) of the SFRO, mailbox. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes)The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tenttive ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CHRISTINA MWANGI's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ Motion For Summary Adjudication As To Plaintiffs Complaint is DENIED. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b). See Notice of Motion and Separate Statement. Additionally moving party failed to cite any law or provide any evidence to support its argument that an owner of the property is not liable for failure to keep premises in a lawful state of habitability. Specifically no support is provided (evidentiary or legal) for the assertions that abatement of Notice of Violation in 77 days is "reasonable" as a matter or law (Note: Notice of Violation indicates that "all items must be completed within 30 days") or that the violations listed in the Notice of Violation are "trivial" not rising to the level of habitably breach. Finally, there is a triable issue of fact regarding Defendant's liability under SFRO 37.10B. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes)Order is signed in open court. (see scanned order for details) Judge: A. James Robertson II, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Gabriella Gutierrez, CSR 024, 415-37-2077, Reported. (501).

Notice Of Motion To Compel Defendant Christina Mwangi To Appear For Deposition And Request For Sanctions
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Ariel M. Gershon of the law offices of Greenstein & McDonals (415.773.1240) appears as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Tessa K. Weeks of the law offices of BledsoeDiestel Treppa & Crane LLP (415.981.5411) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

Both sides stipulate to the judge pro tempore. The cause is argued and submitted by both sides in open court. The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modification.

PLAINTIFFS DALE DUNCAN and MARTA MUNOZ MENDOZA'S Motion To Compel Defendant Christina Mwangi To Appear For Deposition And Request For Sanctions is GRANTED. Defendant Christina Mwangi shall appear for deposition at 10:00 a.m. on January 20, 2017 at Greenstein & McDonald, 300 Montgomery Street, Suite 621, San Francisco, unless the parties stipulate to another date in writing at least three court days in advance of that date, in which case any failure to appear at the mutually stipulated alternative date shall be deemed a violation of this order. It appears that this deposition was continued repeatedly for various reasons, and not that the deponent had failed to appear at a noticed deposition date certain. However, the several month delays in agreeing to a date certain of this defendant appear excessive even under the extenuating circumstances of multiple cases undergoing consolidation and/or trial, and the defendant on balance does not appear to have been forthcoming in offering proposed dates-certain for her deposition, rather than "possible" dates that then went unconfirmed by her. Setting an agreeable deposition date in good faith should be a matter of course and should not require the Court's participation. Here, at a minimum, it appears the defendants' concerns about possible multiple depositions of the deponent before these matters were consolidated, or her alleged unavailability during trial of a separate matter, are now moot in any event, yet it appears to date defendants still have not confirmed in writing a deposition date for defendant Mwangi in this matter. It therefore appears to this Court that defendant Mwangi has not participated in discovery in good faith here with regard to agreeing to a date for her deposition and on that basis sanctions are awarded against defendant Mwangi in the amount of $2,000.00. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge Pro Tem: David Helbraun; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302/jpt)
